name: Ash Chapel Breach
intro: A settlement inside an old quarantine chapel on the Hallowfen road has gone quiet. The bells hang still, but someone has kept a fire inside, and the altar is not empty.
briefingTitle: FIELD WRIT OF THE ASHEN CENSURE
briefing: A hundred and thirty years ago the sky broke open and the damned came down with the wreckage.

What the fires touched did not stay dead. It rose in the shape of Hell the way the old paintings always promised: halos of bone, ribs like chapel doors, mouths full of borrowed scripture. The Remnant names it the Infernal Host. Most people just call it the Host, and bar their doors.

The Bloom that followed should have ended the world. Then, for reasons no priest will explain and no scholar is permitted to, it stopped. People called it the Stilling. The rot went still in the ground mid-reach. The walls held. The bells still ring.
No one is supposed to ask why it stopped. You learned not to a long time ago.

The Holy Remnant feeds the cities, mans the walls, and rings the hours. In return it owns your faith, your confession, and your silence. The sermons call the Host Hell itself come to test the faithful, with obedience as the only armor that holds.
It also keeps a quieter office, the one the sermons never mention. You serve the Ashen Censure. You are a cult-breaker.

When a chapel goes dark, when a checkpoint tower stops answering its bell, when someone starts teaching that the rot is holy, they send you to end it, before it spreads and before anyone starts asking the wrong kind of question.
No sermon makes the work holy, and it has never once felt like saving anyone.
Year after year, the ash road gives you the same work: find the cell, put it down, bury the Censure partner who did not make the walk back.
You have done this for years. You are good at it. It has cost you more than you will ever say out loud.
Lately there are more of them than there should be.

The cults are spreading faster than the Censure can burn them, springing up in places quiet for generations, some with names and rites that match nothing in the old files. No one in the Office will say why. You have started to wonder if anyone knows, or if they simply will not say.
Your latest writ: a settlement inside an old quarantine chapel on the Hallowfen road has gone quiet. The Office suspects a cult. Find out what happened and put the cell down.
You did not expect this one to be where the question finally started asking back.
combatStartBarks: Keep the intruder breathing. The wound needs a witness.
Close the doors. Let the house learn this one.
No clean hands leave the chapel.
The body came shut. Open it.
Hold the aisle. The lesson has teeth.

intro: The cultists turn from the cross, knives still wet with sacrament.
One keeps cutting even as the others rise to meet you.
The refectory cultists scatter their bowls and reach for blades.
One keeps whispering the lesson even as the fight begins.
A throat-singer drags his voice into a wet hymn.
The bent penitent beside him answers with bone-thorn hands.

combatIntro: The cultists break from prayer into violence.

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: the Ash Chapel cell is broken. Ring the upper bell before you follow the wall road.

codex.name: The Ashen Censure
codex.summary: The office you serve. The quiet arm of the Remnant that finds Host-cults and ends them before they spread, or before anyone starts asking why the rot still rises.
facts: Sends cult-breakers down the ash roads on written writs.
Thankless work. Many of the faithful find it distasteful.
Lately the writs come faster than they used to, and the Office will not say why.

codex.name: The Host
codex.kind: The rot. Hell's work, by doctrine.
codex.summary: What the fires of the Descent left behind. It opens a body and rebuilds it in the shape of damnation: halos of bone, ribs spread like chapel doors, borrowed scripture in too many mouths.
facts: Spreads by touch, by blood, and by contaminated relic-metal.
Since the Stilling it no longer catches the way it did in the Bloom. An opening left alone tends to burn out unless something feeds it.
Doctrine forbids asking why the Bloom ever stopped.

codex.name: The Choir of the Open Wound
codex.summary: The cult that took this chapel. They worship the rot and call its spread a kindness. You have never seen their name on a writ. The absence frightens you.
facts: They call infection confession, and the opened ones icons, not monsters.
They open only those they judge to be turning holy. The merely faithful they leave in the road, marked refuse.
They feed a saint slowly, by hand, to keep it growing.
They kept settlement guards for opening and moved the families before you arrived.
Their ledger marks the Hallowfen wall as their next ground.

codex.name: The Penitent Engines
codex.summary: Warrior-monks who cut away their own flesh and seal the rest in machine, so the rot finds nothing to take. The old war-orders, never the Pontifex's. The Censure files them under do not engage.
facts: One stood in this chapel's doorway, then turned and left without coming in.
They do not abandon a route for nothing.
By every record the roads keep, a Penitent cannot fall to the rot.

journalNotes.text: The warden's own book names this place Quarantine House Seventeen. After the Stilling it stopped holding the sick and began holding anyone who argued a tithe or asked the wrong question. The diocese let it be forgotten.
Under-galleries run beneath the chapel, packed jaw to heel with the quarantine dead. The cult chose a house already taught to suffer.
A Penitent Engine stood in the doorway, then turned and left without coming inside. They do not abandon a route for nothing.
Dalia Mor heard one attacker correct another: 'Choir. We are the Choir of the Open Wound.' She counted the name because it frightened her.
The order pinned over the old quarantine writ names the attackers as the Choir of the Open Wound. It says they were holding the chapel until the Hallowfen road went silent.

ambient: Answer clean, road ash. The chapel hears liars.
No closed hands past the threshold.
Chew slow. Let the lesson find the throat.
A closed body is only a locked chapel.
Not this one yet. He still has hymns in him.
Wonder how the guard in the cellar tastes.
The wax remembers every hand that prayed here.
Hush. The Opened Saint is listening.
The wall bells have gone quiet. Quiet gates hear better.
Sing low, or the road wardens hear us.
Smoke goes up. The hunger goes down.
Hold the bowl with both hands.
The fire is never warm enough down here.
When the lesson moves, do not flinch.
The old saints kept their mouths shut. We will not.
No tower bell from the wall. Silence answers enough.
Listen past the chapel stone.
They painted over our prayer marks. I can still see the old lines under it.
Our cups are still here. They drank from them and left blood in the bowls.
The barricade held the wrong people out. It did not hold the knives back.
The wall road is still quiet. Worse after seeing the chapel again.

onComplete.log: Tomas reaches the bell stair and disappears upward.

ambient: No time for shutters now.
They left the pot clean, and a clean pot should comfort me. Not after them.
They slept in our beds and scratched prayers into the posts.
The font is fouled. No one touches it until I boil every cup twice.
Eight dead, you said. I still look for a ninth in every shadow.
They took the clean bandages and left the dirty ones folded like offerings.
I am not kicking it inside again. Not ever.
The chalk drawing is still there. I thought they would cut it up too.

objects.name: The Opened Saint
ambient: Looking down the cross, speak no evil...

interact.log: He is hammered to the cross and still half-alive. The cult has been cutting from him.

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: the altar was prepared to carve sacrament from the guard on the cross.

log: The altar is set for a meal, not a sermon.
Strips of pale flesh lie drying in the bowls. They were cut from the guard on the cross.

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: the ledger names the missing settlement families and points toward the Hallowfen wall.

interact.log: A grease-dark ledger lies open beside a cracked bowl.
Someone has scratched words into the column, under the old whitewash.
A chapel saint carved in stone, the head struck clean off and a wound-star cut into the breast.
A chapel saint carved in stone, the head struck clean off and a wound-star cut into the breast.
One floor flag sits proud of the rest. It lifts on a finger.
Tin cups are set by height around the table. One child scratched flowers into the wood with a nail.
Cold soup has skinned over in the pot. Someone banked the coals before the chapel fell quiet.
Flour, a worn board, and a paring knife. The last meal cut here was bread, enough for a crowd.
The reliquary opens with a dry metal cough.
Jars of beans and hard root sit in careful rows. Two labels are written in a child's block letters.
The satchel smells of ash, old wool, and field antiseptic.
Clean rags hang over the rim. The water is grey with ash, not blood.
Two long benches and tin cups set by height. The cult ate at the settlers' table and left the bowls unwashed.
The barrel is nailed to the floor. The boards beneath it sound hollow.

search.title: Split Barrel
lines: Fresh scrapes ring the nails at the barrel base.

methods.label: Read the fresh scrapes
methods.successLog: The nail heads are bright where metal bit them recently. Someone opened this way after the chapel went quiet.
methods.failLog: The barrel is too scored to tell old work from new.

interact.log: A settlement guard lies on his back in a wide pool of blood, his coat cut open and his chest carved.
A settlement guard lies beside the intake barricade, one hand still closed around a broken chapel key.

search.title: Dead Settlement Guard
lines: Dried blood, road grit, and boot scuffs crowd the floor around him.

methods.label: Read the blood trail
methods.successLog: The blood under his shoulder smears backward. He was dragged to the barricade after the key broke.
methods.failLog: The dried blood gives you nothing useful.

methods.label: Check his boot seam
methods.successLog: A Censure Entry Roll is stitched into the boot tongue, flat and dry.
methods.failLog: The boot seams are stiff with blood. If he hid anything there, it stays hidden.

interact.log: Inside the satchel: a sealed field dressing and old road dust.
A Lumen Compact medicine crate, lid stamped with a clean-hands sigil.
A child drew here in chalk, long before the cult came.
The holy water in the stoup has been replaced with dark blood. A mixture of Host matter and blood.
Black oil, a heavy tread, and a single shed rivet.

search.title: Oil and Tread
lines: The ash still holds the machine's weight.

methods.label: Read the return print
methods.successLog: The return tread cuts deeper than the approach. It backed away in haste, not on patrol.
methods.failLog: The ash is too churned to tell why the machine turned back.

interact.log: The double doors stand open. Cold air drags through the gap.

lock.title: East Watch Doors
lines: The double doors are chained from your side with a small Censure hasp, old but honest.
Beyond them, something heavy breathes through metal.

methods.label: Pick the Censure hasp
methods.successLog: The hasp gives. Both doors swing outward on dry hinges.
methods.failLog: The pick slips and the hasp stays shut.

interact.log: The double doors stand open. Cold air drags through the gap.

lock.title: East Watch Doors
lines: The double doors are chained from your side with a small Censure hasp, old but honest.
Beyond them, something heavy breathes through metal.

methods.label: Pick the Censure hasp
methods.successLog: The hasp gives. Both doors swing outward on dry hinges.
methods.failLog: The pick slips and the hasp stays shut.

lock.title: Warden's Wall Safe
lines: The strongbox is set into the old wall. Grit packs the keyhole, but the pins still sit where they should.
The Remnant plate is a warden's seal, built to slow thieves and frighten prisoners.

methods.label: Use the warden's key
methods.successLog: The warden's key turns hard. The safe opens.

methods.label: Work the keyway
methods.successLog: You clear the grit and walk the pins open.
methods.failLog: The pins hold. Another hard try may chew the keyway.

methods.label: Read the warden seal
methods.successLog: The plate marks diocesan pay, not a reliquary ward. This was a warden's box, not a priest's.
methods.failLog: The seal is too worn to read past the sun-cross.

methods.label: Pry the hinge
methods.successLog: The hinge gives with a cracked metal groan.
methods.failLog: The hinge holds. The noise travels through the stone.

failure.log: Something shifts in the chapel dark, then settles.

interact.log: An iron strongbox set into the wall behind the hung saint.
A hymnal lies split in the old warden's post, hollowed out around a private book.
A Remnant writ is still nailed to the board by the old intake gate.
A stone set low in the old cell wall sits proud of the rest, pried out and pushed back.
A narrow service door is cut into the chapel wall. Behind it, stone steps climb toward the bell chamber.
A worn landing stone points into the narrow stair cut through the chapel wall.
A wound-star is daubed point-down on the nave wall, painted over an older mark.

victoryLog: The chapel falls quiet. No cultist answers the bells now.
